summary: Designs and maintains the digital backbone of the CI‑OS, integrating data, workflows, dashboards, and knowledge systems into a coherent operational ecosystem.
responsibilities: Design and maintain the architecture of the Continuous Improvement Operating System (CI‑OS)
Integrate digital systems including dashboards, workflow engines, process mining tools, and knowledge portals
Ensure interoperability between human workflows and digital systems
Develop and maintain APIs, data pipelines, and event-driven integrations
Implement real-time monitoring and feedback loops for operational processes
Manage digital-twin infrastructure and simulation environments
Ensure system scalability, reliability, and security
Collaborate with Process Engineers and Quality Managers to digitize process parameters and metrics
Maintain metadata standards, taxonomies, and controlled vocabularies across the CI‑OS
Oversee version control, configuration management, and system documentation
authorities: Define system architecture and integration standards
Approve digital instrumentation and telemetry configurations
Modify or deploy CI‑OS components and services
Set access control policies in collaboration with Quality and Security
Prioritize digital enhancements and automation opportunities
inputs: User requirements from all CI‑OS roles
decisionRights: Selection of digital tools and platforms
kaizenCapabilities: Support for frontline and engineering digital tools

constraints: Security and access control must be preserved
All integrations must be documented and version-controlled
